subject: LU-398 ptlrpc: NRS framework follow-up patch

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message:
Patch Set 1:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/12955/

Patch Set 1:

This patch is to address the issues that Isaac pointed out in the original patch, plus some others. I am adding all reviewers from the previous patch, and will post a comment on the previous, now landed Gerrit change in order to reply to the existing comments.

This patch does not yet have the sanity script that Andreas pointed out should be included, but I will add this soon. Also missing is an update for doc/lock-ordering that Oleg pointed out (although that file seems like it might be unmaintained), and also a convention to hold module count usages when policies are registering from other modules.

I will add the above and also set the policies' patches to be a dependency of this patch in the next refresh; I'll do my best to be quick with these patches.

Build Successful

(2 inline comments)

Just a few minor comments.

Patch Set 2: (2 inline comments)

I wanted to add module usage count increases for policies registering by other modules, as they could potentially be removed with curren code while there are requests pending on the policy, in current code; this would lead to a hang. Without the patch to fix this, the work-around would be to only remove the module when all policy instances are stopped.

Also pending is the sanityn script for testing policies, but maybe these two additions can lang in a separate patch in order to get policies landed faster.
Uploaded patch set 3.

The other reason for the delay is that this patch failed the maloo test which leads to the patch being ignored. Now the two bugs that were causing this patch to fail have been fixed so maloo should past now. Try resubmitting again against the latest master.

Patch Set 5:

Of course after I hit submit already, I realized that showing all this hp/non-hp stuff outside of NRS is really unfortunate.
np/non-hp is an NRS form too, so it should be handled inside. I well can imagine cases for policies to give precedence to non-HP requests over HP requests and it's no business of upper layers to dictate any of that (the upper layers will still provide functions to determine what requests are HP).

Might also want to remove the 'nolock' substring from some of the functions, e.g. ptlrpc_nrs_req_get_nolock()/ptlrpc_nrs_req_peek_nolock().

Patch Set 9:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/504a0bce-973d-11e2-ba8e-52540035b04c).  Ran 14 tests.  1 tests failed: sanityn.

Patch Set 9:

I was probably wrong, and this patch is indeed the reason for sanityn test_34 failing. I think this may be caused by not taking this test into account when implementing the new-style op_req_get() API change (Oleg had pointed out that a previous version of this patch was breaking that test, but I think the break occurred from version 6 onwards, i.e. when ptlrpc_nrs_pol_ops::op_req_get() was introduced), which causes some RPCs to be ignored during fail_opc handling near the top of ptlrpc_server_handle_request().

i.e. this patch changes the purpose of ptlrpc_server_request_get() making it equivalent to a request peek() + a delete(), while in current master it only does a peek(). So once fail_opc is hit, another RPC is picked up for handling, and the previous one is just ignored as it has been deleted from the policy's queue.

If this is verified to be the case, perhaps the test could be changed a bit to work alongside this patch, but would it be possible to disable it for now until a proper solution is found?
Uploaded patch set 10.

Patch Set 10:

Thanks for coming up with a fix Oleg, this explanation makes clear sense. I should have worked this one out earlier.

Patch refreshed.

parents.subject: LU-2756 ost: buffer utilization rate of OST rqbd

commit.subject: LU-398 ptlrpc: NRS framework follow-up patch
commit.message:
LU-398 ptlrpc: NRS framework follow-up patch

This patch addresses some outstanding issues that had been raised
by reviewers of the "Add the NRS framework and FIFO policy" patch,
and include some other improvements, e.g. it reworks the API
slightly in order to optimize some frequently-used operations,
does not uanncessarily policies in liblustre and client-only
kernel builds, and makes sure we hold module references when
required, for policies registering from other modules.

Signed-off-by: Nikitas Angelinas <nikitas_angelinas@xyratex.com>
Change-Id: I9306d43e2aef20aa64d6870a56ae99859ce40cd5
Oracle-bug-id: b=13634
Xyratex-bug-id: MRP-73
